deferred_scenarios: R1-5: implicitly covered by WS-2 (valid frame dispatch is tested by agent method success)
R1-7: implicitly covered by R1-1/R1-2/R1-8/R1-9 sequence (state transitions are the mechanism)
R2-1: covered by WS-2 (context summary) + R3-governance tests (policy/budget)
R2-2/R2-3: exec approval flow requires real intent evaluation infrastructure — defer to R2 delivery
R2-5/R2-6: agent.status/agent.wait are thin aliases over sessions.list — defer to R2 delivery
R2-7: sessions.history requires completed sessions with traces — defer to R2 delivery
R2-10/R2-11: sessions.patch and file change events — defer to R2 delivery
R3-2: budget exceeded requires real budget tracking infrastructure — defer to R3 delivery
R3-4: disconnect broadcast is natural extension of R3-3 — defer to R3 delivery
R3-6: reconnect resilience requires full pipeline — defer to R3 delivery

steps.name: Gateway route registration, protocol types, and schema migration
steps.criteria: GIVEN a non-WebSocket HTTP GET request to /api/gateway THEN the server returns 426 Upgrade Required
steps.scenario_name: WS-5: HTTP GET to /api/gateway returns 426 Upgrade Required
steps.implementation_notes: Create gateway domain directory. Define GatewayFrame discriminated union (RequestFrame | ResponseFrame | EventFrame), parseFrame/serializeFrame pure functions, MethodName union, ErrorCode union, GatewayDeps port type. Register /api/gateway route in start-server.ts returning 426 for non-upgrade requests. Create schema migration adding device_fingerprint, device_public_key, device_platform, device_family to agent table with index.

steps.name: WebSocket upgrade handler with connection state machine
steps.criteria: GIVEN a client sends a WebSocket upgrade request to /api/gateway THEN Brain upgrades the connection to WebSocket successfully
steps.implementation_notes: Wire Bun WebSocket handler at /api/gateway. Create connection.ts with ConnectionState type, GatewayConnection record, and pure transition function. On WS open, create connection in 'connecting' state. Frame receive dispatches to message handler.

steps.name: Skeleton auth, method dispatch, and agent method handler
steps.criteria: GIVEN a WebSocket connection with skeleton auth token AND workspace has 2 decisions and 1 constraint WHEN client sends agent method THEN response contains runId, sessionId, and contextSummary matching seeded counts
steps.scenario_name: WS-2: agent method returns runId with context summary
steps.implementation_notes: Skeleton auth: connect handler recognizes 'skeleton-test-token' and transitions to active with hardcoded identity. Method dispatch: pure routing table from MethodName to handler function. Agent handler: validates params, calls deps.assignTask(), maps result to AgentAcceptedPayload with contextSummary. Wire GatewayDeps ports to existing Brain systems.

steps.name: Event adapter: StreamEvent to Gateway Protocol frames with seq numbers
steps.criteria: GIVEN an active agent session WHEN orchestrator produces token events THEN client receives event frames with monotonically increasing seq AND at least one assistant stream event
steps.scenario_name: WS-3: token events stream with monotonically increasing seq numbers
steps.implementation_notes: Pure mapping function mapStreamEventToGatewayEvent covering all 13 StreamEvent variants. AgentTokenEvent→assistant stream, AgentFileChangeEvent→lifecycle stream, AgentStatusEvent→lifecycle, AgentStallWarningEvent→lifecycle, AgentPromptEvent→lifecycle, ErrorEvent→error stream, DoneEvent→lifecycle done. Brain-internal events (TokenEvent, ReasoningEvent, AssistantMessageEvent, ExtractionEvent, OnboardingSeedEvent, OnboardingStateEvent, ObservationEvent) return undefined. Agent handler subscribes to session events via deps.subscribeToSessionEvents, maps each via event adapter, sends on WS with incrementing seq.

steps.name: Session lifecycle: done event completes streaming
steps.criteria: GIVEN a streaming agent session WHEN the agent finishes THEN a lifecycle event with stream 'lifecycle' and phase 'done' is received
steps.scenario_name: WS-4: session completes with lifecycle done event
steps.implementation_notes: Ensure DoneEvent maps to lifecycle/done frame. Agent handler's event loop terminates on DoneEvent (AsyncIterable completes). Remove session from activeSessions set. Connection stays open for further requests.

steps.name: Ed25519 device auth: challenge generation on WS open
steps.criteria: GIVEN a client opens a WebSocket to /api/gateway THEN the gateway sends connect.challenge event within 1 second containing non-empty base64 nonce and recent timestamp
steps.scenario_name: R1-1: connect.challenge event sent immediately on WebSocket open
steps.implementation_notes: Create device-auth.ts with generateChallenge() using crypto.getRandomValues for 32-byte nonce (base64url). computeDeviceFingerprint() via SHA-256 of raw public key. On WS open, generate challenge and send connect.challenge event frame immediately. Store PendingChallenge (nonce + ts) in connection state.

steps.name: Full Gateway Protocol v3 connect handshake with Ed25519 verification
steps.criteria: GIVEN a connect.challenge was received WHEN client sends connect with Ed25519-signed nonce THEN gateway responds with hello-ok containing protocol 3, tickIntervalMs, deviceToken, and role
steps.scenario_name: R1-2: Gateway Protocol v3 connect handshake succeeds
steps.implementation_notes: Add verifyEd25519Signature() to device-auth.ts using crypto.subtle.verify('Ed25519'). Create identity-bridge.ts with resolveDeviceIdentity (lookup by fingerprint) and registerNewDevice (DCR + DB writes). Connect handler: verify signature against pending challenge, compute fingerprint, resolve or register identity, build HelloOkPayload with protocol/policy/auth/workspace fields, transition to active state.

steps.name: Protocol error handling: malformed frames and state machine enforcement
steps.criteria: GIVEN a WebSocket connection WHEN malformed frame is sent THEN invalid_frame error returned AND connection stays open. WHEN method sent before auth THEN not_authenticated returned. WHEN connect sent while active THEN already_authenticated returned.
steps.scenario_name: R1-6: malformed frame returns invalid_frame error
steps.implementation_notes: Harden parseFrame: validate type field exists and is 'req'|'res'|'event', validate id exists for req/res, catch JSON parse errors. Connection state machine: transition() rejects method dispatch in 'connecting' state with not_authenticated effect, rejects 'connect' in 'active' state with already_authenticated effect. All three error paths are part of the same connection.ts state machine — shared implementation justifies bundling.

steps.name: Known device identity resolution from agent table
steps.criteria: GIVEN a device with known Ed25519 public key in agent table WHEN it connects and signs the challenge THEN gateway resolves existing Brain identity and workspace from device fingerprint
steps.scenario_name: R1-3: known device resolves Brain identity and workspace
steps.implementation_notes: resolveDeviceIdentity queries agent table by device_fingerprint, follows identity_agent edge to resolve identity, follows member_of edge to resolve workspace. Returns ResolvedIdentity with isNewDevice=false. Test seeds agent+identity+workspace records before connect.

steps.name: New device auto-registration via internal DCR
steps.criteria: GIVEN a device with unknown Ed25519 public key WHEN it completes connect handshake THEN new agent record created with device_fingerprint AND identity record with identity_agent edge created AND member_of edge to default workspace exists
steps.scenario_name: R1-4: new device auto-registers via DCR
steps.implementation_notes: registerNewDevice: create agent record (agent_type='openclaw', device_fingerprint, device_public_key, device_platform, device_family), create identity record, RELATE identity_agent edge, RELATE member_of edge to default workspace. DCR creates OAuth client_id for audit trail. Returns RegisteredDevice with isNewDevice=true.

steps.name: Presence tracking: query and broadcast for connected devices
steps.criteria: GIVEN two authenticated gateway connections WHEN both are active THEN presence query returns at least 2 online devices with deviceFingerprint, status, and agentType
steps.scenario_name: R3-3: presence query returns online devices
steps.implementation_notes: In-memory connection registry tracking active connections by workspace. Presence handler queries registry for workspace's active connections, returns DevicePresence array. On connect→active transition, add to registry + broadcast presence.update event. On close, remove from registry. Register presence method in dispatch table.

steps.name: Model listing: configured providers without API key exposure
steps.criteria: GIVEN an authenticated connection WHEN client sends model.list THEN response has at least 1 model with id and provider AND response JSON does not contain 'sk-', 'api_key', or 'apiKey'
steps.scenario_name: R3-5: model.list returns configured models without API keys
steps.implementation_notes: Model handler reads config.chatAgentModel and other configured models, maps to ModelInfo array (id, provider, capabilities). Strip any API key fields from output. Register model.list method in dispatch table.

steps.name: Tools catalog, config.get, and method_not_supported error
steps.criteria: GIVEN an authenticated connection WHEN tools.catalog sent THEN response has tools array. WHEN config.get sent THEN response has protocol 3 and features array. WHEN unsupported method sent THEN method_not_supported error returned.
steps.scenario_name: R2-12: config.get returns read-only gateway configuration
steps.implementation_notes: Tools catalog handler: calls deps.listGrantedTools with agent identity, maps to ToolCatalogEntry array. Config handler: returns static ConfigGetPayload with gateway version, protocol 3, features list, tickIntervalMs. Method dispatch: for recognized-but-unimplemented methods, return method_not_supported error. All three are thin handlers sharing the dispatch table — bundling justified.
